heading: Reserve affairs: designation of general or flag officer of each armed force
label: 10203 Reserve affairs: designation of general or flag officer of each armed force
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , prior to repeal by .section 264(a) of this titlePub. L. 103–337, § 1661(a)(2)(A)
Amendments2002—Subsec. (d). substituted “of Homeland Security” for “of Transportation”.Pub. L. 107–296

Effective Date of 2002 AmendmentAmendment by effective on the date of transfer of the Coast Guard to the Department of Homeland Security, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 107–296section 1704(g) of Pub. L. 107–296section 101 of this title
Effective DateSection effective , except as otherwise provided, see , set out as a note under .Dec. 1, 1994section 1691 of Pub. L. 103–337section 10001 of this title
amendments: Amendments2002—Subsec. (d). substituted “of Homeland Security” for “of Transportation”.Pub. L. 107–296

Effective Date of 2002 AmendmentAmendment by effective on the date of transfer of the Coast Guard to the Department of Homeland Security, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 107–296section 1704(g) of Pub. L. 107–296section 101 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: The Secretary of the Army may designate a general officer of the Army to be directly responsible for reserve affairs to the Chief of Staff of the Army.

children.label: b
children.text: The Secretary of the Navy may designate a flag officer of the Navy to be directly responsible for reserve affairs to the Chief of Naval Operations and a general officer of the Marine Corps to be directly responsible for reserve affairs to the Commandant of the Marine Corps.

children.label: c
children.text: The Secretary of the Air Force may designate a general officer of the Air Force to be directly responsible for reserve affairs to the Chief of Staff of the Air Force.

children.label: d
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security may designate a flag officer of the Coast Guard to be directly responsible for reserve affairs to the Commandant of the Coast Guard.

children.label: e
children.text: This section does not affect the functions of the Chief of the National Guard Bureau, the Chief of Army Reserve, or the Chief of Air Force Reserve.
